content.claim: Tracking schools have higher value-added than general schools, while they have deteriorating effect on test scores of students left in normal schools; tracking improves short-term achievement of selected students but impact disappears in the long run
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: One study's finding, not universal consensus
Does not address long-term equity distribution or intergenerational effects
Specific to Hungarian context but limited replication evidence provided
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
